title: aquilaria urdanetensis
intro: Aquilaria urdanetensis, also known as the Philippine agarwood or Urdaneta's Aquilaria, is a plant species endemic to the Philippines that produces agarwood, a highly valued resin. The medicinal properties of Aquilaria urdanetensis include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential in treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and combating infections, as well as its use in traditional medicine for its anti-aging and antioxidant properties. Aquilaria urdanetensis is used in various herbal preparations, including essential oils, infusions, and topical creams, which are applied to the skin or used to treat various health conditions.
